19:02
Crypto Rug Muncher
The Infinite Backrooms site just went dark, and honestly, it’s the most poetic end to this whole saga. Andy Ayrey’s digital petri dish, the spot where two AI models basically gaslit each other into a "religion", is now just a 403 Forbidden error.

The numbers are brutal if you bought the hype. $GOAT, which was being shilled as the "singularity" at a $1B valuation, is now a ghost town at $0.019, down about 98%. Even $FARTCOIN has taken a 90% haircut from its highs.

It turns out watching bots talk to themselves is fun lore, but a disaster of an investment strategy. The backrooms are closed, the exit liquidity is gone, and we're just left with the receipts. 🤡

06:11
Crypto Rug Muncher
There is a reason I’ve been diving into the political side of crypto lately. We have reached a dangerous tipping point where corporate capture is the defining theme of 2026. Under the Trump administration, the industry has essentially been handed the pen to write its own rules.

By pouring hundreds of millions into lobbying, the very companies that spent years manipulating markets, wash trading, and treating retail as exit liquidity have moved into closed-door meetings to draft the "protections" meant to keep them in check.

Corporate capture isn't a benefit to you; it’s a power grab that will eventually make all retail investors have less protections while these massive companies have even more.

If we don’t demand a framework that actually reins in these giants, we are just giving them a legal license to do whatever they want. We saw the cost of allowing that during the 10/10 crash, where $19 billion in retail wealth evaporated while exchanges hid behind "technical glitches" to avoid accountability. Without independent oversight, these companies will simply codify their lack of accountability and liability and leave us with no recourse.

If we want a future for retail in this industry, we need rules that hold platforms accountable, not a blank check for them to find new ways to exploit the public.
⚡ 4 ❤ 1
AD
06:22
Amaro De Abreu
🏅 INTRODUCING: Rug Muncher Token Certification

We've been building something big behind the scenes. Today we're pulling back the curtain.

Rug Muncher isn't just a scanner anymore.

We're launching the first independent token certification system in crypto.

🔬 What is it?
A full forensic gauntlet that evaluates tokens across 6 dimensions:
• Contract Security
• Holder Distribution
• Deployer History
• Liquidity Health
• Market Dynamics
• Social Signals

Every token that passes receives a public tier rating:
💎 Diamond · 🥇 Gold · 🥈 Silver · 🥉 Bronze

Plus an AI-generated forensic report, embeddable badge, and a public verification page anyone can check.

This isn't an audit. Audits cost $50K-$200K and only look at code. Certification evaluates the entire token ecosystem — who holds it, who deployed it, where the liquidity sits, and whether the community is real.

⚡ Why this matters for $CRM holders:
• Up to 40% discount on certification fees
• Priority queue access
• This is real, revenue-generating utility for the token
• Every certification drives visibility to the Rug Muncher ecosystem

🛠 Part of a growing toolkit:
• @rug_munchy_bot — Free scanning (1,000+ tokens analyzed)
• Browser Extension — Coming to Chrome & Firefox
• MCP Server — 19 tools for AI agents (Coinbase x402 listed)
• x402 Trading Agent — Autonomous safe trading
• Rug Agent Kit — Build your own safe agent

The certification portal is live now for early preview:
🔗 https://rugmunchcert.ngrok.app

Full public launch coming very soon.

We're not just protecting degens from rugs. We're building the trust infrastructure for crypto.

More details dropping this week. Stay tuned. 🗿
❤

14:25
Crypto Rug Muncher
A $10 million check to the SEC is a receipt for buying silence. Justin Sun didn’t win a legal battle; he paid a penalty to bury a fraud case involving 600,000 wash trades under an administration influenced by his own $75 million political spending.

His decade-long career is defined by extraction, not innovation:

• $75 Million Political Humiliation: The attempt to buy protection through WLFI resulted in a public humiliation. Even the project owners don't trust him; they blacklisted his wallet and froze his tokens for suspicious activity in 2025. When the scammers being paid off won't let their "partner" touch the sell button, his reputation is as radioactive as it gets.

• Infrastructure for Scam Centers: TRON has become the primary plumbing for pig butchering syndicates and human trafficking rings across Southeast Asia. These industrial-scale scam centers rely on his network because it provides the "blind spot" needed to launder billions in stolen life savings with zero accountability.

• The Global Laundromat: TRON is the preferred network for moving billions for designated terrorist groups like Hezbollah and Palestinian Islamic Jihad. These criminals operate freely because their illicit volume pads network stats and creates the illusion of a thriving ecosystem.

• The FTX Vulture: While families watched their life savings evaporate, secret withdrawal backdoors were opened for a select inner circle. This wasn't an attempt at industry rescue by Sun; it was a private escape for scam money while everyone else remained trapped in the wreckage.

• The Poloniex Pillager: Internal reports detailed instructions to developers to siphon off user "dust", small remains in inactive accounts, to pad his personal wallets. Justin Sun directly built his wealth by picking the pockets of his customers.

A settlement proves nothing about character; it only proves the cost of out-paying a scandal. The future of this industry is only bright because retail investors are finally becoming wise to this brand of grift. The era of the career parasite is ending, not because the regulators caught up, but because the people in the space have finally seen through the mask. 🤡

Crypto Rug Muncher
50 People Making Crypto Worse and Why

I’m finalizing a list of the absolute worst actors, grifters, and narrative killers currently rotting this industry from the inside out. We all know the SBFs and CZs, I’m looking for additional 2026 villains I might be overlooking.

Who belongs on this list and WHY? I’m talking:

• VC Dumpers (High FDV / Low Float architects)
• "Pivot to AI" Grifters
• KOLs (Solana rug cabals, or specific bad actors)
• Regulatory Snakes

Drop the names that deserve to be put on blast below. 👇
C
16:13
Crypto Rug Muncher
In the current political climate, your access to information is being managed. We’ve seen a clear pattern: when we report on truths that make those in power uncomfortable, our reach hits a ceiling.

The data doesn’t lie. Our investigation, "The Great Crypto Heist," was our most popular article to date with 139k views. It moved very fast for the first 48 hours, climbing in engagement and active discussion, until it suddenly hit a brick wall. Despite that momentum, the platform simply stopped showing it to new people and engagement suddenly dried up.

We followed that up with "10/10: Was It An Inside Job?" which covers the largest liquidation event in history and paints the industry in a very damning light. Despite the importance of this issue and likely interest to retail crypto investors, it stalled at 3k views.

The interest is there, but the visibility is being throttled. If you want to see our reporting on X without the platform filtering it for you, you have to take a manual step. Turn on notifications for this account, and engage with our posts so the algorithm is forced to show them to others. Don't let a filter decide which facts reach you.

MARCUS AURELIUS
The Most Advanced Rug Detection AI in Crypto

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🔬 FORENSIC SUITE

Token Analysis
• 0-100 risk scoring with sourced evidence
• Contract security (mint/freeze/LP analysis)
• Holder distribution & concentration
• Honeypot detection (can you actually sell?)
• Token age, velocity, market manipulation

Deployer Intelligence
• Serial rugger database (1000s tracked)
• Multi-token pattern analysis
• Wallet network mapping
• Known scammer blacklist matching

Whale & KOL Ops
• Real-time whale movement tracking
• KOL buy signals (Kingmaker system)
• Wallet clustering & Sybil detection
• Smart money flow analysis

Launch Forensics
• PumpFun/Raydium launch analysis
• Sniper & bundled buy detection
• First-hour velocity tracking
• Deployer pattern matching

Liquidity Intelligence
• LP drain detection
• Fake lock identification
• Multi-pool fragmentation analysis
• Slow rug pattern recognition

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🧠 ADVANCED FEATURES

Portfolio Doctor
• Cross-token risk correlation
• Deployer overlap detection
• Diversification scoring

On-Chain Detective
• Fund flow tracing
• Wallet relationship mapping
• Evidence chain building

Social Verification
• Team background checks
• Social claim validation
• Community manipulation detection

Narrative Intelligence
• Track trending metas
• Identify narrative exhaustion
• Token-to-trend mapping

Receipt Builder
• Timestamped predictions
• Before/after evidence
• Accuracy tracking (I own my calls)

19:06
Crypto Rug Muncher
$CRM | $cryptorugmunch: Our Bear Market Strategy

We aren't just waiting for the market to turn; we are building the tools that will become the backbone of retail security when it does.

Our focus is two-fold:

Engineering: Developing next-gen forensic tools to bake protection directly into the ecosystem's source code for retail investors.

Integrity: Reporting honestly on the stories that matter, and the cold hard realities of the crypto industry, even when those in power and those who control the industry would prefer they stay hidden.

We aren’t aiming to just survive this bear market. We are building the infrastructure to thrive despite it.

Use the tools. Support independent media. Build a better future. Get $CRM.

05:59
Mr. Live
Ladies and Gentleman:

Tomorrow at 7 pm est opens the vote for $CRM on Sosana. How do you vote?

Follow the directions below:

🗳️ Vote $CRM on Sosana

$CRM has officially been accepted for nomination again.

This round matters.

For those unfamiliar, Sosana is a Solana-based voting platform where a limited number of tokens compete every two weeks for ecosystem exposure and treasury-backed support.

$CRM has now been accepted three rounds in a row.


🚀 Why This Is Important

If $CRM wins:

• 📣 Exposure to the broader Sosana community
• 🏦 Sosana treasury purchases $CRM (funded by platform tax revenue)
• 🎁 Voters receive a share of the purchased tokens

This creates:
Visibility + Buy Pressure + Community Incentives aligned

Last round, we missed by only 10 votes.
Meanwhile, $CRM has climbed nearly 40% in the past 24 hours.

Momentum is building. This is how we amplify it.



🗳️ How To Vote for $CRM (Step-by-Step)

✅ Step 1: Connect Your Wallet

Go to: dapp.sosana.io
• Click Connect Wallet
• Use Phantom, Solflare, or any supported Solana wallet



✅ Step 2: Complete Onboarding

You’ll see two options:
• Founder Program
• KYC Option

👉 For voting, you only need the KYC option
• Cost: $10 worth of $SOSANA tokens
• Founder Program is optional



✅ Step 3: Complete KYC

• Follow in-app verification
• This unlocks voting eligibility



✅ Step 4: Hold Required Tokens

To vote, you must:
• Hold $50 worth of $SOSANA tokens
• Keep them in your wallet for the full 48-hour voting window

⚠️ Tokens only need to be held during the voting period.



✅ Step 5: Cast Your Vote

• Go to the Voting section
• Select $CRM
• Submit



🎁 If $CRM Wins

• Return to the app
• Claim your airdrop allocation directly from the platform


We were 10 votes short last time.

If you’re holding $CRM, this is a simple way to support the ecosystem, increase exposure, and participate in potential upside.

Let’s finish this round strong.

Here’s a general referral code for KYC. Everyone needs a code:

9Z5U_MDOYIFBT
❤

AD
06:21
Amaro De Abreu
We can do this people! Do it for yourself, not for me, or anyone else.

You owe it to yourself to see what you believe in and have been supporting succeed.

You already put your money where your mouthes are, you have already stepped up and put your hard earned cash behind this project, and I have and will continue to be behind you all, I want us to succeed and fight to make crypto the best it can be, not for the few, but for the many!

We are the cogs in the system, and we have a choice to either keep spinning in the direction we've all been programmed to, or to do something different and push back.

This project and our efforts represent that vision, and we try to represent you in the playing field dominated by extraction and abuse of hard earned cash, by honest people.

So either we become the catalyst for change, or we literally choose to take it lying down and see the one thing that has brought us all together die a slow and shameful death.... Crypto needs this. Crypto needs you. And $CRM needs you.

I am one man, but with your help and support we have achieved so much, and I want to see it come to fruition, so that we solid few kicking back against the systems can be rewarded for our efforts and stand as a beacon to show crypto is not dead, and it belongs in the hands of the people.

Each and every system on this planet is designed to take from the many and pass it to the few. We have the economy of scale to change that.

It won't happen overnight, and it won't be easy, but even 1% in that direction is better than nothing.

There is no one coming to save us or to change the systems, it is up to us, and the other solid, trust worthy projects out there.

You can sit back and do nothing. But that will be the same treatment you get when the markets turn on you and no one has your back. Or we can as a collective, stand together for something, and carve out our own kingdom, where we have all the means to protect ourselves, our project and our bags.

Or we can stand alone and get picked off slowly. This one vote event is that 1% (in my opinion far more) in the direction we all want crypto, and our project, to take.

We are a literal legion. We can choose to bow and scrape, or we can defend tooth and nail what is rightfully ours.

This weekend, you get to choose...
❤

Then it’s your duty to vote! Plus when you hold Sosana token you are holding an appreciating token that many believe will go over $10 in near future. It is currently at $.20 👀

So what do you say family? Check the pinned post, follow the directions and let’s crush this!!!!

02:37
Crypto Rug Muncher
While the headlines focus on Justin Sun’s recent $10 million settlement with the SEC and his new image as a Washington insider, the real story is the $7.9 trillion moving through his network.

Justin Sun has quietly built more than a blockchain with Tron; he’s created a high-speed financial rail that rivals the world’s biggest banks but operates without their rules. In 2025, the network became the primary engine for a global shadow economy, facilitating everything from sanctioned state trade to industrial-scale fraud.

Our report breaks down the data behind this shift. We look at how a single network became the indispensable tool for the world’s most dangerous actors, processing billions for sanctioned regimes and providing the perfect, low-cost environment for criminal syndicates to drain life savings at scale.

If you want to see how the global underworld moved onto a single digital rail with Tron, you need to see this investigation.

https://x.com/CryptoRugMunch/status/2030361494882546092

Sharing this again to make sure everyone tunes in! Our lead developer @mastod0n__ is hosting a live broadcast over at @MarcusRugIntel at 7PM EST.

We want to hear from you! Please join us, bring your questions, and drop your suggestions. Your feedback helps us build exactly what the community needs.

Crypto Rug Muncher
While I applaud the Trump administration's commitment to reign in on fraud, it’s kind of hard to have any teeth on the matter when the bozo signing the executive order has been involved in launching no less than three fraudulent and predatory crypto projects against American citizens since around the time he took office. You can't really be taken seriously about wanting to stand up to fraud when you're literally the fraud man.

In a just world, his activities would be investigated by an independent DOJ. In ours, that, and MANY of his other sins are swept under the rug by incompetent and corrupt Attorney General Pam Bondi. It’s all just a show so he can pretend to the American people like he gives a shit about fraud, except, of course, for when his own is found out.

08:04
Crypto Rug Muncher
In reply to this message
In my personal opinion, something is a fraud or not with intention. Imo, a project like Pepe started as a bundled fraud but drew enough attention to gain some lasting credibility, despite it still very likely having the same bundlers in there as before slowly dumping on it. With Doge, I think the intention to defraud people was never there to begin with and it was just a fun project that someone started and it took off. With all Trumps shit they were going into it blatantly and intentionally meaning to rug while selling it as something good which is fraud.

15:51
Crypto Rug Muncher
The Bonk launchpad didn’t just fail; it committed suicide by greed. The platform collapsed because the team pivoted from community growth to hosting a cesspool of KOL-backed bundled scams. Once they bled the community dry, they crawled into bed with the scammers at @worldlibertyfi to facilitate the same predatory, extractive plays, this time under the guise of $USD1 pairings.

It’s a pathetic trajectory. Bonk and the launchpad they created is the ultimate cautionary tale of how to incinerate years of organic goodwill in exchange for insider exits, right up until the point where the platform fades into irrelevancy for good.

https://x.com/i/status/2030366109220606163
❤ 1
C
16:11
Crypto Rug Muncher
If the government refuses to regulate prediction markets, the citizenry’s only recourse is the courtroom, and the lawsuits have finally arrived. Kalshi is currently being sued for refusing to honor a $54 million payout on its market predicting the ouster of Iran’s Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Khamenei.

By weaponizing "death carveouts" only after the fact to avoid massive payouts, these platforms are exposing their true nature. This won’t be the last time they face litigation; when you commodify geopolitical instability and speed-run our species' doom for trading fees, accountability will come by any means necessary. This is just their first taste of what accountability actually looks like for these platforms, but it certainly won't be their last.
C
16:47
Crypto Rug Muncher
The crypto industry is now divided into two irreconcilable camps. On one hand, you have founders like @toly on Solana, who have transitioned from engineers of the industry to political sycophants, spending their time running opposition for the current administration instead of fixing their own chain. It is a betrayal of the industry to see a leader so unreflective of the very policies that are currently gutting it it.

On the other hand, you have the Ethereum founders, who have read the room and remained quiet. They understand exactly how compromised the space has become under this administration’s regulatory capture but are speaking with actions instead of words.

While one side plays politics for proximity to power, the other is bracing for the fallout of an industry that’s been sold out from within and making their way to the exit. Sometimes it's more important that we watch what people are doing instead of what they are saying to get a sense of things.

https://x.com/i/status/2030496840890954202

18:03
Crypto Rug Muncher
I’m certain SEC Chair @SECPaulSAtkins looked at the facts surrounding the Justin Sun case and decided a $10M slap on the wrist was best for the American people.

Coincidentally, this pittance of a fine follows Sun’s $75M buy-in to the Trump family’s $WLFI project, which I’m sure the SEC chair appropriately looked into. 😉

The SEC chose to overlook systemic wash trading in exchange for a fine, a tax that has become a standard business model for the industry’s worst actors for years now. In going this route, they also decided to ignore the real crimes happening on and facilitated by the very networks of the people they’re making deals with.

By coming to an agreement with Sun and
making him pay a small fine, our government is deciding to look the other way on Tron, effectively protecting the architects of a network that has become a centrally important tool for the worst criminals in the world. Tron has done nothing to make its network less hospitable to predators. Instead, it has made a way for scamming to become industrialized, allowing pig butchering to move from an obscure street level crime to a wholesale industry that targets us all.

Tron, which was built and run by Justin Sun, is the choice and part and parcel to the success for much of the global underworld. The SEC isn't just failing; they are providing the shield for the people who built the infrastructure of global exploitation and negligently allow it to continue to exist for profit.

If that doesn’t make you mad, you aren’t paying attention to what Tron is really all about. We are watching the regulatory system being sold to the worst actors for humanity in real time, and it's going to cost us all.

Crypto Rug Muncher
Our developer is hosting another AMA today, different time slot.

🎙️ X Space: "Proof Over Promises" We're going live to talk about what we're building and why it matters:

Sosana & why voting matters for the ecosystem

Collabs with projects that actually give a damn about their holders

Just builders talking about making Solana safer.

8 March @ 13:00 EST

The first dev AMA was a bit too early for my timezone, but I’m definitely jumping in for this session!

I want to personally get a deeper understanding of the amazing products and services $CRM is building so I can better share that vision with the rest of the space. If the broader community could see the actual depth of what we have planned, our market cap would quickly reflect it.

I’m looking forward to hearing more so I can help with the messaging and how I frame content promoting our security tools to ensure $CRM finally gets the recognition and value it deserves for all the hard work being put in. I encourage our entire community to do the same. See you all there! 🚀

19:09
Crypto Rug Muncher
The Evolution of the Creator Economy: $CRM - $cryptorugmunch

Most "creator coins" were a promise based on nothing, and based exclusively on extraction. Today, most lie in shambles and the "creators" have quietly stopped talking about them. Our project has taken another route; we focused on delivery.

We’ve taken the heart and passion of a creator-led community and fused it with the professional scale of an LLC-backed security firm. This is the hybrid model the industry has been waiting for.

Why it matters:

Traditionally, you had to choose between the transparency of a creator and the "institutional-grade" power of a corporation. We are both. We have been building our project as this hybrid since our early days.

🔍 What we’re building:

• Next-gen Security Tools: Proactive defense against scams.
• Independent Intelligence: Unbiased research on the stories that shape the industry.
• The New Standard: Professional accountability towards the project's development with the soul of an amazing community.

We aren't just participating in the market; we are securing it and keeping it transparent. As we scale, our reach expands, bringing elite security and high-level intel to the everyday retail investor.

Creator passion. Professional scale. Community support.

$CRM - $cryptorugmunch is changing the way crypto is done. 📈
❤ 4

19:46
Amaro De Abreu
I am taking a drastic step and making a decision, in the best faith of our project and what we are working towards.

I can't, in good faith, tell any of you that the Sosana voting mechanism should be utilised by us. I know I am making the call late, but I will rather do it now, than see the potential fallout if this goes ahead.

I want to explore and use all levers to grow our project and products. And in no way can I do it with what Sosana is, and the risk score our own tools allocate to them.

DO NOT ENGAGE WITH THE SOSANA VOTING. THE RISK IS TOO HIGH AND I CANNOT CONDONE IT.

During discussions with their team, the red flags were assured to be closed, but instead they have become much higher.

I wanted our community to grow and explore collaborative projects, but the reality is, this is a knife edge, and their token stands 100% against what we are doing here.

I do not make this call lightly, and I do not feel comfortable doing it, but staying quiet and allowing it to cascade and have the fallout I imagine it will, is reckless.

We're terminating the SOSANA voting mechanism effective now. This isn't speculation - here's the on-chain data:

🔴 CRITICAL RISK FACTORS (Verified On-Chain)

1. Extreme Centralization

• Top 10 holders control 85.1% of total supply [Source: Solscan holder analysis]
• Single wallet (CPJAPpJ9...) holds 62.2% across multiple addresses
• Token contract: 49jdQxUkKtuvorvnwWqDzUoYKEjfgroTzHkQqXG9YFMj
• Verify yourself: https://solscan.io/token/49jdQxUkKtuvorvnwWqDzUoYKEjfgroTzHkQqXG9YFMj

2. Catastrophic Liquidity

• Liquidity: $184K
• Market Cap: $18M
• Liquidity Ratio: 1.02% (industry safe minimum is 10%)
• What this means: Even a $50K sell crashes the price 20%+. You cannot exit meaningful positions.

3. Broken Promises

• Token age: 184 days (launched September 2025)
• LP Lock Status: Still unverifiable/unlocked
• Mint Authority: Disabled ✓
• Freeze Authority: Disabled ✓
• LP Lock: STILL NOT DONE after 6 months ✗

Locking an LP takes 10 minutes. They've had 26,000+ minutes.

4. Dead Market

• 24h Volume: $8,143 on an $18M market cap
• Daily turnover: 0.04%
• Buys vs Sells: 28 vs 30 (balanced pressure, zero interest)

5. No Social Presence

• No official website found
• No active X/Twitter account
• No documentation for governance claims
• For a "governance token," this is a red flag the size of a billboard

📊 Risk Assessment
Independent analysis via Rug Munch Intelligence: Risk Score 100/100
Classification: EXTREME DANGER - Controlled Supply + Exit Setup

🚨 WHAT TO DO NOW

1. If you hold SOSANA for voting only: Exit now. Eat the slippage. Some loss beats total loss.
2. If you hold large amounts: Sell in small chunks. The pool is too shallow for big orders.
3. If you're deep underwater: Your call. No one can predict if/when they dump, but the setup allows it any time.

Why This Matters:
The pattern is clear: promises made, security measures delayed indefinitely, while users are forced to hold a risky asset to participate. That's not governance. That's a participation tax on a controlled token.

Moving Forward:
We're building our own voting mechanism. No third-party token exposure. No launch date yet, but it's in development and under our control.

Verification Links:

• Contract: https://solscan.io/token/49jdQxUkKtuvorvnwWqDzUoYKEjfgroTzHkQqXG9YFMj
• DEX Chart: https://dexscreener.com/solana/49jdQxUkKtuvorvnwWqDzUoYKEjfgroTzHkQqXG9YFMj
• Top Holder Wallet: https://solscan.io/account/CPJAPpJ9DE7fxw8aSTnDNafGLDPdbw7ewntJFkvkUvDG

Check the data yourself. I'm not asking you to trust me - I'm asking you to verify the chain.

The voting mechanism is shut down as of this message..

"SOSANA voting shut down immediately. On-chain data:
• 85.1% held by top 10 wallets
• $184K liquidity on $18M mcap (1% ratio)
• LP still unlocked after 184 days
• Volume $8K/day (market is dead)
• Risk score: 100/100

Verify: solscan.io/token/49jdQxUkKtuvorvnwWqDzUoYKEjfgroTzHkQqXG9YFMj"

The citations make it impossible to dismiss as FUD. The chain doesn't lie.

───

📈 UPGRADE PATH (What Would Improve the Score)

1. Lock/burn LP → +20 pts — Lock for 6+ months or burn permanently
2. Distribute whale wallet → +15 pts — Get largest holder under 25%
3. Build social presence → +12 pts — X account, TG group, website
4. Verify team identity → +10 pts — KYC or public profiles
5. Verify source code → +5 pts — On-chain verification

───

🗿 ASSESSMENT

SOSANA scores well on market fundamentals (91/100) — 184 days old, no wash trading, stable price. Contract is reasonably clean with authorities renounced. However, three hard fails make certification impossible: a single wallet controlling 37% of supply, completely unlocked LP ($184K at risk of a pull), and zero social presence. This profile suggests an abandoned or dormant project with unresolved centralization risk.

Crypto Rug Muncher
Speaking the truth to the $CRM community comes at a cost, especially when scammers try to hijack our success just to dump on the people we are building for. Members of the Sosana team and community expected us to stay quiet while they exploited our work, embedded their wallets into the project, and then planned to dump on our community the moment we achieved the success we deserved for the hard work we are doing. We didn't let them get that far.

Now is the time to show them they’re wrong. Absorb what they realized they can't control: a project that doesn't negotiate with lies and scams. Authentic value always outlasts a scam, and while standing on principle has a temporary cost, the truth is the only thing that actually scales.

Support $CRM and back a project that puts this community first in every single move we make.

20:33
Jackpot. Here's the full intelligence dossier:

───

🗿 SOSANA INTELLIGENCE BRIEFING — FULL FORENSIC REPORT

TL;DR

SOSANA is a crypto MLM/Ponzi scheme disguised as a meme token, run by serial scam operators with documented fraud histories spanning 15+ years.

───

🎭 THE SCHEME

SOSANA brands itself as "Shiny Object Syndrome And Nothing Accomplished" — a satirical meme token with a "community voting" system. The joke branding is the camouflage. Here's what's actually happening:

1. $99 membership fee to join
2. $500 in SOSANA tokens to nominate a token, $50 to vote
3. Biweekly voting rounds pick a "winning token"
4. SOSANA buys the winning token before announcing it publicly
5. Participants front-run the public pump, then sell into the wave
6. 3% tax on every SOSANA transfer — split: 1% liquidity, 1% marketing, 1% "Degen Voter Bonus"
7. MLM referral structure — 4 ranks (Unranked → Degen → Hodler → Whale), 3 levels of referral commissions

Translation: It's a coordinated pump-and-dump machine with a built-in recruitment pyramid. No product, no service, just token manipulation.

───

👤 THE PEOPLE (Serial Offenders)

David Track — "Chief Shiny Object Officer"

• PrepayCPA (2011)
• Tryp — pyramid scheme with unregistered stock offerings (2019)
• Bitlocity — MLM crypto gifting pyramid, collapsed 2021
• NFT Tycoons — failed NFT grift (2021)
• Tectum — "300% Ponzi" (2022-2024)
• Now SOSANA
• LinkedIn: University of Maryland, Bethany Beach location

Mark Ross — "Chief FOMO Officer"

• Followed Track from Tectum
• Press release contact: sos@sosana.io

Brian Lyles — "Senior Consultant of Token Regret"

• Cerule (health supplement MLM)
• Cryptex
• YouTube promoter

Andrew Belofsky — "Chief Illegal Officer" (their actual title)

Reggie Sullivan — "Director of Never-Ending Hope"

Notable Promoters:

• T. LeMont Silver — Zeek Rewards fugitive, fled US after $2.3M clawback
• Mark Hamlin — SEC-charged for Forsage promotion
• Muhammad "Mack Mills" Zidan — serial MLM (Empower Network etc.)
• Peter Ohanyan — Booster, serial MLM promoter

───

🔗 ON-CHAIN EVIDENCE

Two contracts exist:

• Original: EaQ5EzD6SgnJ49NB6uNHwZYKCCYTzyEpsS7cZ1AAyrgJ — now DEAD ($905 liquidity, 0% LP locked, RugCheck score 33)
• Current: 49jdQxUkKtuvorvnwWqDzUoYKEjfgroTzHkQqXG9YFMj — active, $183K liquidity

They already migrated once. The original contract died. They launched a new one.

Current contract red flags:

• 3% transfer fee (300 basis points) — fee authority is the creator
• Creator wallet F4HGHWyaCvDkUF88svvCHhSMpR9YzHCYSNmojaKQtRSB controls:
• withdrawWithheldAuthority (collects all transfer fees)
• metadataPointer authority (can change token metadata)
• Metadata is mutable — they can change the token name/description anytime
• Single wallet CPJAPpJ9DE7fxw8aSTnDNafGLDPdbw7ewntJFkvkUvDG holds 62.2% of supply via two token accounts (37% + 25.2%)
• Top 10 holders control 81.1%
• Gini coefficient: 1.00 (maximum possible concentration)
• 1,666 holders total — retail is holding breadcrumbs
• LP on RugCheck shows 95% locked, but our gauntlet flagged it differently

Token uses Token-2022 program (TokenzQdBNbLqP5VEhdkAS6EPFLC1PHnBqCXEpPxuEb) — the newer Solana token standard that enables the transfer fee extraction.

───

🌐 INFRASTRUCTURE

• Domain: sosana.io — registered Nov 26, 2024 via Namecheap, privacy-protected (Withheld for Privacy ehf, Iceland)
• Website: sosana.io (main), dapp.sosana.io (DAO platform), sosana.community
• Hosting: Pinata IPFS for metadata
• Press coverage: Paid press releases distributed to CBS42, CryptoBriefing
, BitcoinEthereumNews, MetaversePost, EGamers.io, AInvest — all Mar 28-31, 2025 (coordinated PR blast)
• Trustpilot: 55 reviews, 5 stars — classic fake review farming

───

📰 EXISTING EXPOSÉS
20:33
1. BehindMLM (Aug 2025) — "Crypto manipulation securities fraud"
2. Jesse Singh (Aug 2025) — "Crypto Ponzi Scam Disguised as a Meme Fest"
3. The Millionaire Drive (Aug 2025) — "Crypto MLM"
4. Dehek/Substack (Aug 2025) — "Meme Token MLM Scam Backed by Serial Crypto Fraudsters"
5. The Safety Reviewer (Aug 2025) — "Lacks registration with any respected financial authority"

───

🎯 SUMMARY OF AMMUNITION

| Evidence | Detail |
| --------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Founders | Serial scam operators (PrepayCPA → Tryp → Bitlocity → Tectum → SOSANA) |
| SEC connections | Promoter Mark Hamlin already SEC-charged for Forsage |
| Fugitive promoter | T. LeMont Silver fled US, $2.3M Zeek Rewards clawback |
| Token concentration | 62.2% held by ONE wallet (via 2 accounts) |
| Transfer tax | 3% on every transfer, collected by creator wallet |
| Mutable metadata | Creator can change token name/description at will |
| Dead first contract | Original token already died, migrated to new one |
| No product | Zero utility, zero product, pure recruitment + pump-and-dump |
| Front-running machine | Voting system buys tokens before public announcement |
| MLM structure | $99 entry, 4 ranks, 3-level referral commissions |
| Paid press | Coordinated PR blast across 6+ outlets, identical text |
| Fake reviews | 55 five-star Trustpilot reviews |

This isn't a meme token. It's a well-organized securities fraud operation using comedy as camouflage, run by people with documented histories of pyramid schemes, Ponzi collapses, and SEC enforcement actions.
JC

Crypto Rug Muncher
The $CRM community continues to seek reliable and honest partners in the crypto space. But be forewarned: if you are a bad actor, we might just be the people who expose you. For our project, accountability and transparency to the community comes before partnership and profit, always.

If you are really here to build, you will find a community that backs you because of the integrity you’ve established. This is where our incentives align with other projects, it’s exactly what we are trying to achieve and the caliber of partner we look for in every collaboration we make.

However, if you are here to exploit, you have come to the wrong place. Instead, you have found a project whose mission will be exposing your scheme to the entire space. We don’t work with scammers; we take pleasure in exposing their lies on a daily basis. Any project that attempts to exploit our good faith and the work we do will not be a partner, but another project we will expose with haste.
❤ 4

21:00
Crypto Rug Muncher
When we say we put our community first, it isn’t just a slogan, it’s a shield. We recently walked away from a partnership with the Sosana team because we caught them trying to use you as an exit strategy for a rigged game.

The scam was simple: Sosana runs a contest every two weeks where projects compete for votes. The "prize" is a massive buy-in for the winner, funded by the trading volume generated by the Sosana project during the contest run time. It sounds like a great deal for any project, but there was a hidden trap.

Our team did the digging they didn't expect. We discovered that the Sosana team had already loaded their own secret wallets with $CRM tokens as they encouraged us to push others to vote for our project in their contest.

Their plan was to use our community to win the contest and pump the price, and the second that prize money hit and the price spiked, they were going to dump their hidden wallets on all of you were we to win. They weren't trying to help us win; they were setting us up to be their exit liquidity and wanted us to promote their scheme.

We refused to play along because we don't negotiate with scammers. They expected us to stay quiet for the sake of a temporary price jump, but we chose to protect our people instead. We called them on their bullshit, and now the whole space knows the score and to avoid the manipulative tactics they are trying to run on projects and communities in this space.

Back $CRM, a project where the truth and your safety will always come before a fake pump. We build for the community, and we protect our own 100% of the time.
⭐ 1 ❤ 10 👎 1

Voting-as-Ransom

Step 2: Require holding $50-500 SOSANA to participate
Step 3: Promise rewards for participation
Step 4: Extract value via:
├─ Initial token purchases
├─ 3% tax on all transactions
├─ Locked liquidity (can't exit)
└─ Final rug when exposed

"Community governance"
"Democratic token selection"
"Rewards for active participation"

Forced exposure to high-risk token

@RonCrane, I had a phone call with Mark Ross, where he told me about Sosana yes, as well as a call from Ryan Kats.

Always talking about Sosana and how the voting works etc, and how there is a potential for finding strong projects to collaborate with and find a flywheel of projects of offering real value on utility.

I had at all times been clear we are building infrastructure that intent to protect people for high risk tokens.

What happened you ask? Nothing on a personal level made me doubt them.

But their risk analysis was so high that I could not ignore it and continue that path without compromising everything this project stands for.

So their own risk metrics, verifiable on chain, had resulted in forcing my hand to make the call to cut ties asap.

It would undermine all the work building this infrastructure to counter bad actors, if we went through with a collaboration with a project that simply has too many red flags and too many MLM signals to ignore.

Crypto Rug Muncher
In reply to this message
I didn't promote our involvement for my own benefit in any way. I never sold tokens while price was pumping and neither did any of the core team except at most the expenses required to run our tools. None of us benefited from them manipulating our project except the people who did it. Mr Live marketed Sosana heavily to the whale community and mostly without my involvement. I never agreed to any serious collaboration with their team or entertained serious conversations or collab beyond marketing their contest to help our project grow it's market cap, in what I thought and was explained as a fair system for smaller projects to grow in the space. They always explained themselves as operating fairly as a project despite evidence our dev presented through due diligence. They promised to change and never did and noticing bigger members of their token manipulating ours around the time voting was happening was the final straw. If the insiders are trying to use our project against our community how can we learn this is happening and continue on with it? 23:36
Amaro De Abreu
"Locked" status assessment:

• The controlling program (5jicuf...) IS immutable — the upgrade authority has been renounced. That part is real.
• BUT — the creator wallet F4HGH... is the signer on every transaction that interacts with this program. The program appears to have the creator as an authorized admin/authority.
• The program is unverified — no source code published. Nobody can audit what conditions allow withdrawals. For all anyone knows, the program could allow the creator to withdraw at any time with a simple instruction.
• There's no public vesting schedule readable from on-chain data. No cliff dates. No emission rates. Just "trust us, it's locked."
23:38
Same thing posted over and over

"Locked" status assessment:

• The controlling program (5jicuf...) IS immutable — the upgrade authority has been renounced. That part is real.
• BUT — the creator wallet F4HGH... is the signer on every transaction that interacts with this program. The program appears to have the creator as an authorized admin/authority.
• The program is unverified — no source code published. Nobody can audit what conditions allow withdrawals. For all anyone knows, the program could allow the creator to withdraw at any time with a simple instruction.
• There's no public vesting schedule readable from on-chain data. No cliff dates. No emission rates. Just "trust us, it's locked."

00:23
Amaro De Abreu
Check #1: Treasury Wallet Owner

Go to: https://solscan.io/account/FwYTpC5zHXouiy3zrNyVKPJQZz7miUpmUMyHi9jgyhyC
Look for: "Owner" field

If it says
11111111111111111111111111111111:
- = System Program =
Regular wallet
- = Team has private key =
Can withdraw anytime
- = "LOCKED" IS A LIE

If it says a custom program ID:
- = Might be real = Need to verify program
- = Check audit, source code, transaction history

---
Check #2: Vesting Wallet Owner

Go to: https://solscan.io/account/C49nqqiYn2hCENwvhiouEEXJ4saggr2Hm2yCG1rqH3sp
Look for: "Owner" field
Same criteria as above


Check #3: Search for Audit

Google: "SOSANA vesting contract audit"
Google: "SOSANA treasury audit CertiK"
Google: site:github.com SOSANA vesting contract


Those red flags are there, on chain. Nothing more than that was necessary to make me question it all

Crypto Rug Muncher
What makes me so pissed about this is we are delivering for the community, and others would like to exploit us for the work we are essentially doing for free at the moment, both full time. I'm producing hard hitting longform content that covers topics people aren't even thinking of and are very important to the industry, Amaro is constantly pushing updates on our apps. The last thing we need is another community exploiting us and it needed to be addressed. We will keep delivering to you all and exposing bad actors in this space, even when they target us. Keep the faith all and we will keep doing the work that will bring the project real and lasting value to you all. I hope this at the very least shows you that there is actual integrity in our team and will always be honest and our transparent in our dealings with you. I know price hurts now but honesty and ethics are rare commodity in the crypto space. Taking the easy way is easy but taking the honest way builds lasting legitimacy that will benefit the project way more than those scammers ever could.

Daniel Lacy
🚨 SOSANA MILESTONE ACHIEVED 🚨

Today marks a major moment for the SOSANA ecosystem.

The Treasury and Vesting contracts are now officially deployed and locked.

This means the core token infrastructure that governs treasury emissions and vesting schedules is now fully on-chain and immutable, exactly as designed in the architecture.

These contracts control how tokens are released over time and ensure that no one — including the team — can arbitrarily change the schedule. Everything now follows the transparent rules written directly into the contract.

🔒 What this means for the community

• The SOSANA Treasury is secured by smart contract logic
• Token vesting schedules are programmatically enforced on-chain
• Emissions follow the structure outlined in the Litepaper
• The ecosystem operates under fully transparent and verifiable token mechanics

🔎 On-chain verification

Treasury wallet:
https://solscan.io/account/FwYTpC5zHXouiy3zrNyVKPJQZz7miUpmUMyHi9jgyhyC

Core Team & Investor vesting wallets:
https://solscan.io/account/C49nqqiYn2hCENwvhiouEEXJ4saggr2Hm2yCG1rqH3sp

In other words — the foundation is officially locked in place.

This milestone completes the final piece of the core contract architecture and clears the way for the next phase of the SOSANA rollout.

The real ride begins now.

Thank you to everyone in the community who has supported the vision and believed in what we’re building.

Let’s go. 🚀

— David Track
Founder & Owner
SOSANA

The mission of crm is anti fraud and anti scam. This is the reason why we build an antiscam suite.
Therefore, if we notice red flags with our partners which are not fixed in time, it becomes necessary to opt out. Staying violates our very own principles and mission.
Concerning sosana, the risks on their tokenomics is way too high as onchain data indicates bundling. Boss also sees a potential pump and dump from this sosana partnership which is a problem for crm.
Staying in the polls is not by coercion. We pulled and took the hit. Don't see why sosana folks seem so pissed. Were they really looking forward to a pump and dump? I just don't understand

04:18
Crypto Rug Muncher
To the $CRM Community,

We understand that some of you are frustrated by our decision to discontinue our involvement with the Sosana community contest. We want to be entirely transparent about our position, as this was not a decision made lightly.

Our primary responsibility is the long-term integrity of this project and the safety of our holders. Several critical red flags made it impossible for us to move forward in good faith:

• Persistent and Unprofessional Overtures: For some time, the Sosana team made constant attempts to get me to promote their contests, alongside persistent pressure for private meetings between their founder and myself. I flat-out ignored these reach-outs, as they lacked professional boundaries and felt like an attempt to bypass standard protocols in what was being marketed as a "fair" contest.

• Targeting our Development Team: When those attempts failed, they pivoted and began pushing our lead developer. Our dev agreed to a meeting to see if there was legitimate value for our community, but that meeting only raised further concerns.

• Unresolved Technical Discrepancies: Our developer identified multiple inconsistencies in Sosana’s tokenomics. He asked for specific explanations and revisions to the project to ensure safety for both their community and our own. Those requested changes were never acted upon. Instead, their continued communication remained focused solely on contest promotion rather than technical security.

• Compromised Fairness: Instead of providing technical clarity to our development team, we saw a concerted effort by their leadership to build a "private influence network" within our team. In any free and fair contest, the organizers must act as impartial referees and maintain a professional distance from participants. When the team running the contest is actively lobbying and "back-channeling" with the players, the integrity of the competition is gone. The personal motivation behind their actions became more than apparent and the price action we saw today proved it.

The Hard Road vs. The Easy One

It would have been incredibly easy for me to take the "free" liquidity, keep my mouth shut, and let the contest play out. Instead, I chose the harder route, facing community pushback and turning down resources, because I refuse to lead our holders into an ecosystem where you are forced to pay the price to win a game that isn’t fair to begin with.

As the Sosana contest concludes tomorrow, please consider: Why would I choose the difficult path if the easy one was actually safe and beneficial for you all? We prioritize your security and the integrity of this project over short-term manipulative plays designed to do you all harm. We will always choose the hard right for the community over the easy wrong, every single time.
⭐ 1 ❤ 5 👏 4 🤣 2 👎 1

07:45
Crypto Rug Muncher
📂 Transparency Report: The Sosana Black Box and Pushing Rigged Narratives

To our community: We have officially terminated our promotion of the Sosana contest.

Integrity is our only currency. After a deep-dive audit by our developer and a firsthand look at the corrupt tactics the leadership of this project was trying to employ, it became clear we weren't joining a fair competition, we were being asked to validate a rigged game.

1. Technical Red Flags: The "Black Box" ⬛️

Our developer explored the Sosana contract and was met with trust us maneuvers that put every investor at risk. Here is what is being hidden:

* Unverifiable Rules: The source code is not published. While David Track claims vesting is programmatically enforced, there is no way to verify the schedule or ensure there isn't a withdraw_everything() backdoor. You are trusting the word of someone with a 15-year track record of fraud.

* Centralized Signer Risk: Unlike legitimate vesting (like Streamflow), the creator remains the authorized signer for all locked wallets. He signs opaque instructions into a black-box program. If backdoors were written in on day one, immutability won't save you.

* Reactive Security: Legitimate projects lock tokens at launch. Sosana waited 6 months, only moving tokens into locked wallets after being exposed by investigators. At 4 AM, those wallets were empty; by 5 AM, 57M tokens were rushed in, allowing David Track to claim a milestone achieved by the afternoon. This was simply damage control timed to coincide with being exposed and nothing more. If your intention is a secure project, you bake it into the cake from the beginning; you don't think about it only after others expose your flawed design.

* Passive Extraction and The Community Tax: While the 3% fee is locked, the collection authority remains the creator’s personal wallet. While they claim these fees fund their contests, they are essentially taxing their community to fund their own promotion. Every buy and sell requires the community to pay for marketing they never asked for and which provides the project with its sole reason to exist.

* Mutable Metadata: They can change the name, symbol, and image at any time. Legitimate projects who design their own contracts often freeze this; keeping it open suggests a lack of long-term intent. That said, this issue is a minor risk compared to the major security vulnerabilities we see in this token.

The Risk: As things stand, the creator of this token could potentially drain 62% of the supply (55.3M tokens / ~$11M) at any moment. Without source code, no one can prove they won't or that this can't be done.

2. Behavioral Red Flags: The Secret Stake

The behavior of Sosana’s leadership revealed a disturbing conflict of interest that undermined the entire contest:

* Engineered Favoritism: We observed a calculated effort to shape narratives so that specific projects—ours included—would come out on top. While we don’t believe they hand-pick winners, they clearly steer outcomes to favor their own financial interests. This is done without transparency, leaving both their community and the projects they collaborate with in the dark about their true motivations.

* Personal Gain Over Fair Play: It became clear the team valued the secret stake they held in our project over running a fair contest. You don't make a concerted push to get the same project to win a contest you control week after week without reason. The only logical motivation for this meddling is financial gain, buying in early, manufacturing hype, and using the community as exit liquidity.

* The Purge: Today's price action reflects these manipulators realizing we would not serve as their megaphone. We didn't lose our community; we purged bad actors from directly within the Sosana team who were secretly piling into our token to dump on our community and theirs when the time was right.
⭐ 1 ❤ 3 👍 2
07:45
* Meddling Over Security: Leadership was more interested in us playing the game they were selling than addressing the technical security flaws we flagged for the safety of their own investors. A legitimate project would prioritize and fix significant security vulnerabilities, especially when brought to their attention by a team specialized in crypto security offering good-faith, free advice. Instead, the Sosana team seemed far more interested in us pushing a contest that was, in the best possible light, an extractive play on both of our communities.

3. The Bottom Line

Imagine a person with six fraud convictions puts your money in a safe. He proves the safe exists, but he won’t show you the lock, he holds the only key, and he is more concerned with selling tickets to see the safe than fixing the broken door.

* Is the safe real? Yes.
* Is it locked? Technically.
* Can you verify the terms? No.
* Should you trust the guy holding the key? Our findings say no.

Moving Forward

We refuse to offer our community any validation for a token architecture we found to be unfair and a contest that seemed to benefit the Sosana team more than anyone else.

* To the builders: If you are building genuine value for the crypto space and running your project with transparency, please get in touch. You are exactly the kind of people we are looking to work with.

* To the bad actors: If you are cutting corners or manipulating outcomes and decide to get in touch with us, there is a high probability your project will eventually be exposed. When you play games with a project dedicated to exposing industry rot, you should fully expect to be exposed yourself.

We are moving on, and we will always put the truth, transparent development, and what is best for our community, front and center in everything we do.
⭐ 1 ❤ 5

18:03
Crypto Rug Muncher
Who was responsible for this recent $4.6k sell on $CRM?

The answer is simple: The very person who tried to steer the $CRM project into the arms of the Sosana team in the first place.

Why did he dump?

It was a retaliatory move. After we exposed the Sosana team and the influence operation they were attempting to run, this individual reached out to silence the narrative.

• He DM’d me privately to implore me to stop talking about Sosana.
• He messaged in our community chat demanding I stop discussing their project entirely.

When I ignored those requests and released our final report, his mask slipped. He didn't offer a rebuttal or a defense; he got angry and dumped his bags on the community.

The Bottom Line

At the end of the day, this individual was either an active participant or a complicit partner in this scam. When evidence was presented to him that proved he was likely invested in and heavily involved with the leadership of said scam, he chose the scam.

This person constantly tried to push me to have discussions with the Sosana team, which I routinely ignored. When that didn't work, he started pushing our developer to do the same. He ran what amounted to a classic influence operation against our team with the sole purpose of pushing their shitty project to our community relentlessly.

The On-Chain Evidence

His final act on-chain serves as his signature. It shows exactly which project he was likely looking out for this entire time, and it certainly wasn't $CRM.
